So far, I like this purse a lot.  I have no negatives to report so far.  I like to have different pockets & zippered areas to put things in because I don't like to have all the stuff I carry in one pocket.  That way it's easier to find things and keep things organized.  It's great!

Due to the great price I found in my size, I ended up buying three pairs of these shoes. I returned one, that was just due to me not liking the look of black patent on them though. I kept the pairs I ordered in blue and white though to go with spring and summer dresses and skirts that I have, trying to dress up my wardrobe a bit so I dont rely on flip flops all the time.  I rank these as running somewhat small, but not terribly so. I am about an 11.5 in shoes, kind of wide. I bought these in a 12 because they were available (which is not super common for this size) and I didn't want the issue of my toes possibly going over the front (not a cute look), and I'm glad I did because they fit perfectly for me. So I would say maybe go half a size up and order direct from Amazon or a seller that has free returns so you can easily send back if the sizing doesn't work out for you.  My one negative, and this is due to my weird toes more than anything, is that my second to last toe has a habit of poking out the side of one of the straps. But not a big deal .  I haven't worn these for an extended period of time, but then I typically don't wear dressy shoes for long. I just don't have many occasions that call for it. But for the short amounts of time that I have worn them, they've been pretty comfortable for me.

Really nice tunic top with hoodie, perfect for the colder weather. I live in northern Florida and I am always looking for warm tops that I can use during fall/winter/spring evening wear. This tunic is a perfect fit (I am 5'7" 160 lbs and I got a size large), it's soft and attractive, long enough to look great with leggings and is light enough to wear in 60 degree temperatures and also warm enough to wear in days and evenings colder than that. I really love this tunic and highly recommend!

I am so sorry I ordered this. The romper itself is loose fitting and generously sized for an XL. An XL on me would normally fit perfect or tight depending on the cut. However, this fits nice and a bit loose. It feels comfortable on and has a front tie and two generous side pockets. HOWEVER, this is totally see-through. In addition, it has that terrible polyester feel. It is made in China of 65% polyester and 35% cotton but I would never know there is any cotton in this garment.  This also came with a broken piece of jewelry that does not open. It has a clasp that is permanently affixed to the end of one of the loops on the chain and the piece that hangs down has a broken link that is just permanently twisted in place. I suspect this is supposed to be a wristlet where the attached clasp attaches to the broken link but I cannot test that because the link does not exist.  Although the jewelry should be thrown out, the romper can be worn if one does not mind baring all their essentials. My rating of 2 stars is only for the romper as a result of being see-through and having an awful polyester feel. I did not add my rating of the jewelry because it seems some people got a wristlet and others did not. If I had added this into the review, I might have rated this 1 star.  This romper is not what I expected. I suspect the other colors are a lot less revealing. It is an attractive outfit, otherwise.  I will not be wearing this no matter how comfortable this is.

For color, I would it give 5 stars...AWESOME with a tan. It is a darker coral than shown in the photos... more like a neon coral. But I like that. AND let's all support MADE IN THE USA!!! However, I continue to struggle, trying to figure out is Ujena sizing... I have ordered several different suits... Reading other reviews, I ordered 2 sizes bigger on top, to achieve that looser look shown on the model.. I am a 38DD and the 3x fits in the bust area nicely, however the bottom portion of the top is about 4 -5 inches too long, more like a form fitting swimdress, not a tankini.  The inner lining, meant to be a miminizer, is almost as loose as the outer top, so I wouldn't count on that to help hold you in.  I don't need it so I will probably remove it and possibly hem the top. Another note, if you're super modest, you should know the bust portion, while lined, is thin. "Nips" are visible. As for the bottom, I normally order a 1x however with Ujena I have learned the bottoms need about 1 size up...usually, however, these are so large I couldn't wear them in a pool without a "wardrobe malfunction." Plus, the little ties on the side are cute and are functional to scrunch the sides. They draw attention to your thighs. So if your confident in that area, go for it.  I am ordering a different style bottom as I am not really thrilled with my thighs.  :-)  Listen, no woman's body is exactly the same, and so, I will continue to shop Ujena because I can buy the larger top and smaller bottom.  One day, I will figure out the mystery of their sizing and I will share it!!

I am not impressed at all by this wallet. It feels cheap and the center section where a folded bill or two can be kept looks like it can easily be ripped apart.  This is a super minimal design. It has a front ID window with room for one card behind that. In the center, there is an opening on two sides where a credit card or few folded bill can be stored. On the back side is room for four more cards. In addition, it has RFID protection that provides unwanted prying from RFID Scanners but I have no way of verifying that.  This feels super cheap to me; like cardboard cover in cheaply designed leather. The leaf logo on the back is nice, however. Currently, there is no price on the Amazon site; however, I do not feel this is worth more than $3 to $4. Overall, I am disappointed because most of the other Auliv wallets I tried are really nice and some are super impressive.

These HIRUI COPPER COMPRESSION SOCKS (3 Pairs; for Men Women, Medical/Athletic Fits) do NOT seem to be the equivalent of standard 20-30 mmHg compression socks.  My disabled husband uses[[ASIN:B07GVPZQSS Nuvein 20-30 mmHg compression socks]] on a daily basis, and the Nuvein socks provide a tighter fit and are much harder to put on than these HiRui socks.  The HiRui socks are advertised as giving 20-30 mmHg compression at the ankle (and foot?) only.  Supposedly the HiRui compression is 15-20 mmHg at the calf, and 10-15 mmHg at the top.  I suspect that the compression at the ankle is more like 15-20 mmHg, and that the leg and calf compression is much lower than advertised.  The HiRui socks seem to provide only slightly more compression at the calf than plain Starter crew socks.  The “copper” fabric is definitely made of woven thread (see my photos), but it is probably just “copper-colored” thread (without any metal).  The copper color looks like it might be dyed on, but the socks are actually double-knit with copper-colored thread used in only the outside layer of the socks.  These HiRui socks are very inexpensive (about $4 a pair on Amazon).  Although they don’t seem to be true compression socks, they may be somewhat helpful as “traveler’s socks” (socks intended for people who must sit during long airline flights).  Choosing the right size of these socks is pure guesswork.  The seller recommends L/XL for women, and XXL for men.  One wonders how the socks can be expected to deliver the advertised amounts of compression when they come in so few sizes.  Standard compression socks generally come in a range of several sizes for both women and men, and the sellers suggest that you measure your ankles and calves to find the best fit.

I am a longtime Brooks wearer, so when I was offered the chance to try out Mizuno's Women's Court Tennis Shoe, I jumped at the chance. I have a lot of foot issues including severe arthritis and overpronation, so was looking for a shoe that would offer stability and comfort. (Sometimes I am able to wear shoes in a regular width, like Reebok, so I was hopeful that the Mizunos would fit).  Out of the box, the shoes looked and felt great in the hand, with a striking purple and white color theme.  Unfortunately, from the moment I put them on, it was instant discomfort. I have hallux rigidus as well as tailor's bunions on both feet, which means my bone spurs protrude quite a bit. Not only was the shoe too narrow through the toebox, but through the entire middle as well. It was uncomfortable even having them on for just a couple of minutes. There was no mention of the shoe being narrow on the box - only 8.5 (and looking on their website, it does not appear that they manufacture this model in wider widths, or I would be willing to give it another try).  Too bad, as the shoes looked and felt very promising on initial inspection.

I own so many pairs of these slipon Skechers.  I love them all. For the review I would say that they fit to size... not too small or too large.  I will say that they do tend to stretch a bit after you've worn them for months.. I find this only makes them easier to put on. However, everyone's foot is different, so I would honestly suggest you try on a pair to find just the right fit.  It was easy when there was only one "type"... now they have so many different styles and I find they each fit differently.  This particular style features "memory foam" soles and they are particularly comfortable because of that.  They feel like you're wearing a really high quality slipper around.  Very lightweight.  Very soft.  I am waiting for them to come out with a really lovely coral color!  I do like that these black shoes do not have the light gray stitching as accent.. they are all black, including the soles.  Now the inside is a really vibrant neon red/pink. I Just received my Rebecca Minkoff phone case/purse. I have to say for me,  I’m disappointed, and here is why:   1. With the fringe hanging down its hard for me to get to my phone to open the magnetic flap, then get the phone out of the case inside before it rings out.   I’m not a fan of the fringe, and might like the look of this case better with out the fringe. 2. The fringe on the underside is more of a bright reddish color! 3. I have a iphone 7 plus, it is not in a thick case, but The pocket is tight, although   there is a cut out in the leather to push it out! Still not easy to get the phone out for me, and I do not have long nails, although manicured. 4.It is a deep redish brown. I like the rich brown color better that it shows in the picture. I notice the case color changes in different light! 5. The strap is a gold chain about 16inch long on both sides, then the top that goes over your shoulder is leather to match the case, and is 14 inch. The strap is a Total of 3 feet long, and heavy.   This hangs down to far for me.,although this is cross over strap that I do not like, as the chain is heavy, and cuts across the middle of the bust. (I'm Not big busted) You can take the chain/strap off. It hooks on both sides, then you have  A small clutch wallet with fringe.   For me when I go shopping I put my small purse on the counter, take my card out to pay, the point is with this   Heavy chain strap, it's going to be fall off the counter, and is noisy on a glass counter.   I'm probably more picky then most here, but I'm a shopper, I have a big family.  The Inside of this case folds over. When open it has the pocket for your phone, below is a zipper for cash, or change. Then you have 4 slots for your lic, credit cards etc..  I do Not carry a large purse but I cannot get my single car key with the remote car fob inside the case.  This comes in a box with the picture of the bag on the cover of the box. Then you pull out the inside box, like a Dvd to get this case out. The top of the box has a hanger on the top. I personally have never seen a $100.00 case handing on a hook in a department store!

UPDATE BELOW...........After reading other reviewers reports and reviewing the sizing chart, I purchased the xl.  I usually wear a 14/16 or 0x/1x.  I am 5'7".  They fit comfortably snug. If you're a loosey goosey undie wearer, I would size up.  I went out on a limb and ordered the black, the gray and the beige (or nude - it is a very yellowish beige).  It was risky for sure.  They are mostly cotton with a little spandex for comfort.  They washed and dried nicely.  They are interestingly sewn, where some thought was truly given in construction.  There is a 1/2" elastic waist - which is zig-zag sewn to keep it in place.  The crotch is a wide, but possibly a little short.  They are high waisted, over my belly button and fit snugly - maybe a slight bit of flattening, but they are not meant to be girdle undies.  The older I've gotten the more I am into comfort and the so called "granny panties."  Whatever works.  The black is a deep, true black - the gray is heathered and the beige/nude is a little yellowy or buttery to me - just a little. Overall, I like these undies. I included some pics of the gray pair because it seemed to be easy to see the stitching, etc... Having said all that, I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful! UPDATE:  11/30/2020.... Ok.. after wearing these for a while I am back to purchase more.  I wear the gray the most so that is what I buy.  I am not very fond of the neutral/nude/beige color.... it's yellowish to me.  So I am sticking with mostly gray and black - but that is personal choice.  They have washed and dried beautifully multiple times.  Snug, but comfortable.  Holds your tummy in just a little.  Stay up.  Honestly, these are some of the best cotton undies I've purchased in a while! UPDATE:  12/4/2020... purchased a few more.  These are really good so far.  Hope they last as well as they fit!

There have been many skechers versions.. this one is one of my favorites. The back of the heel is soft - not tight - doesn't slip and cause blisters. I think that Skechers are mostly about comfort and there is a style for just about everyone - this one works perfectly for me. I really like the black with the black soles. The uppers are a stretchy fabric... the bottoms are very light rubber like material - that have been molded. They do have some tread - no skid and NO squeaks. I was looking for nightgowns that had a defined cup area/something to keep a chest in check and I decided to give this a try since it looked decently supportive. I lucked out by finding a color variation in my size that was much less than the rest. I paid $11 and change for it and honestly? the gown is just fine, but I wouldn't pay more than that for it.  The Pros: the chest section is made out of this stretchy, relatively soft lace. Im actually a size 16-18 right now and usually get an XL in nightgowns, I took a chance on a Large in this though since it was pretty cheap and the sizing worked out great for me. Im a 38C cup, and the fabric does a good job of keeping things front and center. If you have boobs, you know what I mean by that. This was the main thing I was looking for in a nightgown, so for that alone, I will rate this 5 stars. If you are wanting something with coverage though, this isn't for you. It's very sheer.  The Cons: This just looks cheap. I got it in pink and the material is very shiny. I saw that the other colors are ranging from $35 to about $48 and I can't believe that anyone would pay that much for this nightgown. No way. The material in the body of the gown is also pretty sheer as well, but you can see that from the picture. It is kind of a weird combination, between the see through lace on the chest and sheer, shiny fabric and the length of it, its like it can't decide what style it wants to be. Its a demure length with sexy elements to it. I'm 5'10" and it comes to the bottom of my kneecaps, so it's pretty long. It just seems like it should be shortened a bit or something. There is just an odd feeling about the style. But overrall I am happy with it. If the other colors were as low as the first one I bought, I would pick up a couple more. But not unless the price dropped significantly.

This was a highly anticipated spring item for me… Only, when it arrived it fell far from expectations. Loving the Capri pant with paper bag waist and in linen no less- I was excited. (my trouble spot is through the middle.. thx to x4 kids and gravity.. ugh!) As for the Capri cut- I realize this is affected differently by height.. but, in leui of a Capri pant these wore like floods- Only the material at the cuff continually is twisted causing such a ruckus.. the twisting is pulling up on the waistband- possibly, someone with a different stature would have better results but, start to finish I felt like a mess- definitely, not cut for my body type or my expectations For reference: I am 5’2”, 31.5-32” waist, 152lb., 38C.. I ordered this in XL and it didn’t do the trick!   I gave this 2 stars: x1 due to the fact my body type may not fit the overall cut and style. I cannot hold that against anyone... and x1 for stitching detail seems to be on point as, it does not appear sloppy or, to have loose threads. I hope this info can help your decision… Feel free to let me know!

I don’t know what made me think this swimsuit would be even the slightest bit flattering, but I was wrong. The swimsuit is double layered and it has removable padding in the top. I cannot keep the padding in the top and smooth. It likes to fold in half and escape during the wash. The arms being off the shoulder do not work at all if you have any type of bosom. I can pull it up and have the sleeve go over my shoulders, more like a cap sleeve, and that does keep it from completely falling off. This bathing suit feels both too big a too small all at once. I am 5 months postpartum. I wear a size 12 and I am 5’4”. This suit feels very loose on me. Like, the fabric doesn’t even try to hold any amount of me in. The fabric just feels very loose. But then the height is really short. Again, I’m 5’4” and it is barely tall enough to cover my chest. If I were a couple inches taller, you could forget it. The top of the suit hits me exactly where it hits the model. However, I have a DD chest to hold up, so it is VERY barely covering. It does have a shelf bra, but without straps, exactly what good is the shelf going to do for you? Even wearing it cap sleeve, which is very barely comes up high enough for that, it just doesn’t offer enough coverage. Smaller sizes, smaller ladies will likely have entirely different experiences. But this is certainly not the suit for this mom. I do love the floral pattern. I do love the inner pink lining fabric. It is fully double lined, but it doesn’t feel like it when you have it on. Maybe I needed a smaller size? I’m not entirely sure where it all went wrong here.

I could only give this a 2 star as it keeps time, so far!  The packaging looks good.Till you open the box to this HUGE Thick cheap looking Watch. The silver part, Bezel of the watch, does not look like sterling as its suppose to look like. It looks like plastic to me!  The Stop Watch tells the seconds every 1 seconds. So you could not use this if you where really timing any type of event.  The top stem you press in to start the stop watch. Then Push it in a second time, it stops the watch with out reseting it.  The bottom stem resets the stop watch to zero.  The calender on the watch has a space to give the date.  Its a battery run watch.  I have not tested this for being waterproof, as my Husbands other waterproof watches all have a screw Bezel which this one does not have!.  The band is a thick stiff leather type.It does not look like its real leather at all.  There is a Warrantee that comes with the watch , that you can buy for about $60.00 extra .Based on the tag price of $595,00 for the watch!! This watch is only worth $60.00, and looks like a 60.00 watch!  I gave it to one of my Grandsons (23). He said it was not that comfortable (big and band is stiff), but would give it a try!  I really wanted this watch to look good, but I was disapointed!  "Up Date" My Grandson has been wearing this watch for a couple of weeks. He said its more comfortable to wear, and does keep good time. He now likes it. That said, I still think it looks cheesy. For a young man of his age Its a good sport watch. For a business man, I don't think its nice enough / appropriate.

I am 5'8"..  38DDD and usually buy a 14/16, 0x or 1x.  I have purchased this brand before and know that a 2x works best for me.  This blouse was no exception in sizing.  I read the the other reviews and I've included a picture.  I took the pic with natural daylight coming from behind me.  I have a "nude" bra on.  Honestly, it is a little see through, but I don't feel inclined to have a wear a cami underneath.  I think it is a personal choice.  The blouse is well designed to be flattering on a lot of different body types.  A few words on the design... the strip under the breast portion is very snug... but it is positioned in such a way that it is too empirish.  The bottom of the flutter panel in the front of the blouse is actually sewn down, so that reduces the look of a maternity top. I also like that the v front is not too low. You can actually manipulate the v a little to make it even less revealing... or more... your choice.  The sleeves are nice and loose and hit me at the elbow, as shown on the model. The material is very soft.  Fabric content: 95% rayon 5% spandex... wash instructions say: hand wash cold, with like colors. Do not bleach. Lay flat to dry, low iron when needed.  I wash in my machine on delicate/handwash and I have a dryer "closet" that I actually hang it to dry with a "shake" cycle... so I don't need to iron. I also bough this blouse in black. I am really glad I did.  Both could be dressed "up" or worn with jeans.  I think they will both be good, versatile pieces. I always update with any issues that might be helpful. UPDATE:  I LOVE these blouses.  Very, very well designed for the larger woman.  They washed up perfectly and I've worn the black one on a plane trip I recently took.  Heavenly comfort and yet.. with style.  Love love love.

So... I am 5'8"... 38DDD, usually wear a 14/16 or 0x/1x - I purchased the XXL. It fit perfectly at the bust - My body shape is mostly hour glass, but I think this dress could hide a bit of a tummy... and the rather high scoop neck definitely modest - although that isn't a thing for me - I do appreciate a bit less cleavage this days at my age (57).  I am not an "Amazon woman"  (pardon the pun) in height.. but this dress is about 3" too long for me. Easily remedied with a hem, so not to worry.  The sleeves are as advertised, three quarter length - nice to hold the wobbly parts that happen when you age.  And the pockets!  They are long and deep.  That means USABLE pockets - not just decoration.  The material is rather heavy-ish.  It's a poly with a little spandex.  I just wish I like the print.  In person it's very ... dowdy.  I will be donating this dress. With a sad face though. Because I LOVE the idea of the dress - just wish for better, more modern (?) prints?? It machine washed nicely and I tumbled dried it on low.  The label listed that dry cleaning was also an option.  Fingers crossed for some better or no prints! I couldn't knock stars because I personally didn't care for the prints. It appears to be well made and easy to care for AND has those dreamy pockets... Just didn't work for me in person.  I included a couple of pictures of the print, one up close so you can see the colors... it's really a mix of orangy red and some pink with a dark teal predominately.

The photos I took were at an open window to allow in natural light.  It is a deep blue - I would say a Navy blue.  The handles are rolled - and hard.  It also comes with a cross body length, adjustable strap - that attaches with swivel clips - a quality touch. It has all the outer appearances of a very high quality bag - just no expensive logo. Unfortunately I need something that can go off roads into the wilderness & snow drifts bc that’s where my service dogs prefer to walk. Lol.

The top of the sock has about a 1/2" is a bit tighter knit... I don't find that they slip down... maybe my legs are bigger than others who have had "slipping" issues.  They come nicely packaged in a gift-type box... rolled as the first pic shows.  The next pic I put my fingers in to show the spreadability of the cuff. The last pic I put a tape measure on so you can see the size of a relaxed sock.  All in all I find them comfortable to wear. I would purchase them again.  Having said that, I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.

I really wanted to LOVE these... really glad I only purchased one to try first.  They are very difficult to set.  I spent over an hour to "adjust" just one side!  The magnets are very strong - good and frustrating.  * make sure you keep your two shoes apart while you adjusting because the magnets will marry to the closest magnet.  Another hint, set them WAY WAY looser than you think you need.  It's easier to tighten in adjustment than to loosen.  I initially tried one piece on a different pair of sneakers and because of how the tongue laid, I couldn't use these.  So... unlace the magnet and start all over. OH NO!  After going through this I feel that having a second person help while you have your shoes on (standing up so you can get the right tight on) would be best in my opinion.  They say - you can take them and reuse them over and over again.  I want to cry when I think about having to move to another pair!  Which brings me to the price.  YIKES.  I think it would be less disappointing at about 1/2 the price - currently it adds $20 to price of your pair of shoes.  So, as long as the pair of shoes I have these currently set on have soles, those magnets are ON.  I didn't cut the laces off as suggested just in case I decided NOT to continue - but in all reality laces are fairly cheap.  On the other hand, waste not want not.  I purchased the white to go on some NB black and white sneakers.  It "looks" ok.  As for the fit... I am not able to get that nice snug lace fit.  The other part of this is that if you try to get them really snug, like you would laces, they will pop right open as that is the magnet's clue you're trying to take them off.  You do still need to bend over and attach them together - in case that is an issue for you.  And as I said before I highly recommend having a second person help to get the perfect fit. I am so tired of tightening.. OH NO too tight. Readjust... OH NO it's too loose. Over and over again. Having said all that, I can't really recommend these.  Sort of like trying to build a better mouse trap I think.  I've tried so many of the "no tie" options.  So far, this set is one of my least favs due to the very frustrating installation that I can't quite perfect the fit without a second set of hands.  Eh.  The video makes is look like a breeze, but in my experience, quite a bit more difficult than shown. UPDATE  5/15/2021  I just purchased something I can't believe is so easy Stretch laces.  Works like I had HOPED these would!  Tie as you like and leave it tied.  They give just a enough to slip them on.  NO MORE magnets that snap to each other... no way to comfortably adjust to the right tightness on your foot... you don't need a second person to help adjust to that comfort level...  I just tossed the magnets.  So glad I only bought one pair.

I was really looking forward to this blouse. However, I find myself giving it only two stars for the color and cut/style in hand being identical to the pictures. I really like the pleated styling on the chest and sleeves. Sadly, that's about it for the pros.  No where online or in the packaging or on the shirt itself is there list of the materials used. I should have seen that as a warning flag but I loved the design and color so I got it anyway. I have no idea what this blouse is made of, but I can tell you that it is *very* itchy! I have never worn anything that has annoyed my skin so badly. Not even 100% wool directly on exposed skin itches this much. The size is way off as well. I got a large which, according to the description, is supposed to fit a 44" bust. I am a solid 36DD and I thought by getting the large I was sizing up (I usually size up as I really do not like skin tight clothing) but it turns out that their size chart must be wrong. The large fits like a medium would on me and I normally purchase and wear a large in similar blouses and tops. Going by their size chart, I would need to size up to a 2XL at the least.. or probably a 3XL.  Bottom line... if it wasn't for the mystery itchy material and the fact that I just can't bring myself to buy, let alone wear, a 2 or 3XL, I would maybe consider giving this blouse another try as I really do like the style and the color. If you decide to buy this blouse, I *strongly* urge you to size up *at least* 2 sizes, perhaps more, and be prepared to wear it as a layer over another shirt to protect yourself from the material.

I ended up ordering 10 pairs of lounge pants from Amazon. I swear, it wasn’t intentional. I was looking for a lightweight lounge pant for fall and late spring in the northeast. I ordered 4 pairs at first and was disappointed by all of them, so I ordered more. Thankfully my next batch had winners. Here are my reviews and rankings.  (1st Place) Ritiriko Women's Joggers with Pockets, High Waist Yoga Capri Pants for Running Workout Lounge: My favorites! I pulled these out of the bag and I thought they would be too tight and small. The waist was the perfect fit and it managed to be fitted without feeling tight. The material isn’t cozy or super soft but it is smooth and very comfortable.  (2nd Place) SPECIALMAGIC Sweatpants Lounge Wear for Women Ankle Length Loose Fit Casual Pants Baggy Workout Active Cropped: My second favorites! I am 5’4” and these hit me right at my ankle but could also be pulled up. These were SO SOFT and the waistband was comfortably loose. These are a lower-quality dupe for Boody Eco Wear lounge pants. I actually returned the Boodys because for $60+ I expected something softer. Something like this! Of course, Boody’s lounge pants are made of bamboo and sustainable, whereas these are rayon, so, of course not ideal, but so dang comfy and only $20. Also, I think these look a little better on than pictured. I got it in black.  (3rd Place) Yogipace Petite/Regular/Tall Women's 7/8 On The Fly Pants Drawstring Casual Lounge Joggers Travel Sweatpants: I reaaaally liked these. At 5’4” the 27” length was perfect. These had a little more weight than most other pants. Very sturdy waistband. If you don’t like cozy soft and prefer a smooth, heavier soft (hope that makes sense), these will be good for you. These are nylon. The waistband on these was pretty loose and there’s lots of slack in the drawstrings for adjustment. You might want to consider sizing down, and you definitely want to if you have a small waist.  Amazon Brand - Core 10 Women's Yoga CoreCloud Fleece Jogger: These were a great, comfy, cozy, fleece-lined lounge pant but were too warm for what I was looking for. These would be great in the winter. I didn’t keep them because I already have winter lounge pants. Very similar to the Ann Taylor LOFT Outlet Super Soft line. The price point is about the same if you watch LOFT Outlet sales. I prefer the LOFT Outlet style because they are cozier and the waistband is thick and high-rise.  KEEPBEAUTY Womens Active Yoga Sweatpants Loose Workout Joggers Pants Comfy Lounge Pants with Pockets AND DIBAOLONG Womens Yoga Pants Wide Leg Loose Drawstring Comfy Lounge Workout Sweatpants with Pockets Cameo M ^ These are the same! The exact same, folks. Lol. So the material of these was suuuuper soft and cozy, lightweight, loved it. The catch was the waistband. I found it a little tight and uncomfortable. And it didn’t have a drawstring to loosen. This wasn’t a sizing issue, because the legs were loose even on my thick thighs. If you have a slim waist you should def get these. If you don’t you might want to avoid.  CRZ YOGA Women's Stretch Lounge Sweatpants Travel Ankle Drawstring 7/8 Athletic Track Yoga Dress Pants: These seemed to be high-quality and were very comfortable without being cozy soft. I definitely wondered if I could get away with wearing these to the office; they were very flattering. These would be perfect for wearing over short shorts or a bathing suit in the winter on the way to the gym or studio. I am not keeping them for lounge purposes.  Sarin Mathews Womens Yoga Pants Pleated Wide Leg Loose Comfy Lounge Pants Workout Sweatpants for Women with Pockets: These are a really soft, comfortable material and fit beautifully. You could totally get away with wearing these to the office. If you practice yoga and don’t like how fitted typical yoga pants are, these are a great alternative. I don’t think the model photo does these pants justice. Watch the product video to see how they really look. Again, though, they were tight in the waist for me. I don’t mind this amount of tightness when I am out and about but I wanted something looser for lounge. I could have sized up but the pant legs were already a tad too long on me.  BALEAF EVO Women's Skinny Cotton Joggers Sweatpants Tapered Casual Lounge Jogging Pants Fitted Yoga Workout Pocketed Pants AND BALEAF Women's Cotton Sweatpants Cozy Joggers Pants Tapered Active Yoga Lounge Casual Travel Pants with Pockets ^ Again these are the EXACT same thing. Even though I ordered these in the same size, one arrived with an inch larger waistband than the other. I don’t know which one is which because other than that they are identical, so, sorry, can’t advise as to which one you should get if you want the larger waistband. Lol! I would wish you luck in figuring it out, but honestly, these aren’t great. The material is rough and the seams are unattractive. The Amazon joggers are a much better buy.

The first thought I had when I tried this on was "How many umbrellas needed to die to make this shirt?"  Nylon is a bad idea for a shirt material. One benefit that it does have is that it is noisy and rustles well and makes and interesting sound when you slap your chest with cupped hands gorilla style.  Literally, that is the best thing I can say about it.  My girlfriend said, and I quote, "There is not a single thing I like about that shirt."  And that was even after I showed her the gorilla thing... Perhaps it would be good for messy eaters as it probably would wipe clean with a rag, otherwise, why?  Do not recommend anyone ever make a shirt out of this stuff again.

Watch died after taking a shower with it on.  LCD display blank, no hand movement.  Claim is water resistant to 10 bar, but shower resistant to 0 ft!  Condensation due to temp diff / humidity may have caused internal moisture & short.  Outside Amazon.com return window, so now I have the pleasure of dealing with Casio Warranty.  11/15/2010 UPDATE:  Sent watch to The Time Machine in Montrose, CA for warranty repair per Casio's instruction.  I got a bill for $91.14 to repair the watch.  They said a small crack near the lower left screw in the case was the reason for the moisture leak.  The warranty doesn't cover damage to case.  I probably received the watch with this defect but never noticed this hairline crack.  Thanks a whole lot Casio for a worthless warranty and a watch that only was good for 2 months.  11/16/2010 UPDATE:  Amazon to the rescue!  As a last resort I called Amazon.com and described the ordeal with Casio and their repair center deciding not to cover the watch under warranty after only 3 months of ownership.  As a courtesy, Amazon to extended the return window, and they shipped a new replacement watch to me overnight which I now have.  Thanks Amazon.com for stepping in and doing the right thing when the manufacturer would not and keeping me a loyal customer!  6/26/2011 UPDATE:  Second watch case today completely separated with the band from the rest of the case (weak points near the screws on the plastic case).  This is not a durable watch design as the plastic casing has cracked on two of them in less than 1 year.  I don't recall hitting the watch on anything that would have caused this damage.  I know Casio will not cover this under warranty, so I'm just going to cut my losses on this - and never buy a Casio watch over $10 again as this design makes it virtually a disposable watch.

Really liked this in theory but, using this for a face mask/cough cover against covid and all day or, extended period of time wearing… is not really feasible. This still has a way to go to make it truly workable-  I’m like every other mom… searching for the right Covid face mask for our family and especially, for our kiddos. With a high school teen, who’s less then, happy about “our new normal…“ I’m spending time this summer searching for different options. I am hoping with some trial/error, to find the best solution prior to the return(?!) of in class high school- whenever that maybe.. ha ah ha!!  This is a neck gaiter that functions like those commonly used in winter.. It is a solid sewn circle, (think turtle neck- without the shirt attached!) of stretchable fabric (11”-15” height) this is pulled over the head and bunched up around the neck. As needed in cold (or, in this case.. germ exposure) you slide up the bunched material from around the neck to properly cover the nose/mouth area.. this also has a filter pocket sewn in, which I like and is my favorite part! For obvious reasons, the pocket section is to be aligned over mouth/nose to keep virus and partials out.. or, let anyone else’s germ particle in..!! The materials stretch allows you to adjust mask into the comfortable position.. and due to the spandex, the mask will stay in place. These state appropriate use for warmer weather… (can even be used to block the sun.) Only, Im not sure that is entirely possible with this. The use of synthetics fabric alone by default, increases the temp/sweat produced. The only way I see this style being truly possible- is using a linen/gauze… Something thats breathable- only then, we are defeating the purpose..?!! (See- good idea theory just has a long way to go still.. Or, save for colder weather.) Unfortunately, it looks like we may need to keep searching- Upon trying the gaiter mask myself, I noticed how much bigger issue… The fact I wear prescription glasses, this would not allow me a comfortable fit with the glasses stem over the ear. The mask material kept tweaking and re-positioning my eyeglasses.. and not in a good way! If, I pulled it down to leave enough room on the ear, I found the mask wanting to roll in that position.. making it more of a hurt then, help! Something to keep in mind for those who are looking at these who also use eyeglasses!! Over all, the idea is there- it just needs to be tweaked and therefore, I don’t recommend for use as a cough/germ cover. The protective inserts that came with the mask are the most valuable part of this set in my opinion. Good luck everyone in their search to be safe.. you are not alone! I hope this info helps- feel free to let me know!

The texture is so perfect, not usually the case with  leather - there is always a flaw somewhere... on the flip side, perhaps this is just a really fine quality. I am hoping that it is real and will become as supple as my old bag with some wear and use. The stitching seems really good.  I also hated the skinny strap of my old bag... the new bag has a lovely strap that is about an 1" in width... for me, just right - and so much more comfortable.... but everyone has a personal preference.  I like that I can shoulder it or crossover, which I do when I need my hands free - doesn't bother my back as much as just shouldering it. There is plenty of adjustment on the strap for the hang length as well... I am about 5'8" and there was spare length at about low belly/high hip. When you open the flap there are a couple of magnetic envelope pouches, the largest fits my new LG G4 perfectly, three slots and a zippered area.  In the main body of the bag there are two zippered organizers and a couple of side slots, that would hold a very small cell phone, keys fobs or other things you want to reach quick - too short for reading glasses, though... The bag itself isn't terribly heavy which is good. When I "load" it with everything I need it's still reasonably weighted... 3-5 lbs (?) It came with a storage/dust bag, a nice touch, although it will probably not spend much time in it. It arrived quickly (I have prime) along with some other things I had ordered.  I will be updating  with any wear or other issues as necessary.  I will try to include some pics if they get past the censors and they include my old bag for reference as well as a 12" ruler for comparison. 8/27/2015... I have been using this bag exclusively for a few weeks.  It's STILL my fav.  I should also add that if you wanted to you could slide in a kindle or an IPAD if you wanted to. I have the larger Kindle and when I am going somewhere I know I will be waiting, I slide it in.  I am seriously considering buying a second one to have a backup when this one gives out, hopefully years from now.  :)  Could go with a different color... but honestly I usually buy the black as it goes with everything, all year.  But that's just me. I did get a like smudge of something grayish/white.  I used a little leather cleaner and wiped it off, a very slight amount of black come off too, but didn't leave any noticeable trace.  So I am ok with that.  It has NOT left any color on my clothing.  And I have been in damp area with rain/mist and/or sweaty humidity - so... I think that is good.  I will continue to update with any issues or other thoughts.

Imagine the most inviting blanket of softness and someone makes you a comfortable lounge set of clothing out of it- this is just that! This is one of the softer materials to snuggle up too- Only, when you put this on are you met with an outfit of issues and disproportion- And try as I might, like Cinderella’s sister to make this softness fit- it didn’t work for myself!! Just be aware of what to expect with this little number..  The material is so inviting! You feel a warm, worn in goodness in this synthetic fabric.. it has enough fuzzy to be soft- without any feeling of fake fur or, a matted mess. It is made of a thinner material so, it’s not too heavy and easy to move in.. The synthetic fabric itself contributes to it’s ability to stay warm. You’ll find the bottom edge of pants are cuffed, keeping them in place and waist line is elastic with a tie feature to fasten for a personal fit...  Only, the fit is my problem!! The top is a hooded cropped pullover style that has left bottom edge 100% raw! The shorter length and thinner material combined with this unfinished edge- let’s this top begin to roll up when wearing.. and not in a good way!! this took place with the outfit in the largest size possible.. Now, the pant has a high waist (as shown in photo..) and unless, you are Barbie herself- I am unsure how these pants would ever fit?! As the legs are plenty long- they offer some room for the hips and thighs before they taper down to the bottom.. but, it seems the roominess is placed in the wrong spot- disproportioned? The problem starts in the waist with it being really narrow and maybe too high? *appropriate curves placed in specific areas on these pants just feel off.. the width for the hips feels out of line for the high waisted effect- Yes!  I’ve worn high waisted before- Ha! I am one who DID live the 80s fashion in the 80’s..!! and these measurements don’t seem realistic- unless you’re without female curves it the appropriate places- being these are adult sizes, they should account for at least some hourglass shape. **NOTE: Part of the problem can be MY lack of height.. and therefore, this may fit some incredibly taller, long torso’d person a bit better?! I will try on my college age “ tall drink of water when she arrives !!” It just didn’t seem to match up on any level for myself or, what I would consider the norm. or, expected.. average!?  •I deducted 1 star for the raw edge that causes the material on this top to roll.. it needs weight to keep top raw hem in place and has none.. I deducted 1 star as well for the pant construction issues as well. If you find yourself interested in this top/bottom set.. you’ll need to be prepared to mix/match/find sizing that may work for you- bc, of this dilemma… I do not see or, recommend this as a good gifting idea! TBH, this seems hard enough to match fit for oneself… Let alone, guess for another person. I hope this info can help your decision… Feel free to let me know!  UPDATE: because these were such a wrong fit for my short/squatty stature- I gave them to daughter who was home for holidays.. she fit in them so so well!! 5’6”, 140lb, 36C... 32” inseam, athletic build. She loved how soft and comfortable these felt. **Only- after wearing about 3hr. I noticed as she was lying on floor in front of fireplace- she had a tear in the material.. But, she had a lot more than that going on!! Christmas lounging and nothing else was the cause.. Wow! TBH, she’s devastated by the outcome since, these are single-handedly the softest most inviting pajamas she ever remembers putting on. I’m not certain if the stitching missed the edge of the material? It’s almost as if the loose fabric cannot hold together the actual stitching of two pieces? I have included photos and keep in mind this is after wearing for a few hrs. And not washing at all... sad to say, with better quality construction- these would be knocked out of the park..!!! I looked and saw another review had the same issue on a sleeve seam?  Maybe this is hit or miss… But, before I endorse I want to make certain it is something you all are aware of!

This ARABELLA WOMEN’S SHORT ROBE (Black, Large Petite) fits my 5’ 3” size 10/12 figure perfectly.  When I wear it, it looks exactly like it does on the model in the product page photo.  (You do need to tie the inside waist tie—common on wrap robes—and thread the sash through the “belt loop” at the front edge, if you want the robe to hang properly.)  However, this robe seems quite expensive (about $32 on Amazon) for what it is.  It’s a simple wraparound garment constructed from heavyweight rayon jersey (a knit fabric).  The only seams are at the neckline and armholes.  The knit fabric edges, including the lower sleeve edges at the wrists, are NOT hemmed, but are “finished” with loose machine overcast stitching.  All it takes is one broken thread to start the unraveling of this stitching.  There is no embroidery or other decoration.  There is no hanging loop.  The robe is NOT particularly warm, and it’s complicated to put on (especially because the knit jersey fabric collapses into a heap whenever the robe is taken off your person).

Wow.  If I could rate this blouse a 10 star, I would.  I am 5'7"... 38DDD... usually wear a 14/16, or 0x/1x.  The xx-large is a perfect fit... could get away with an x-large... maybe... I suppose if you're petite...shorter... you MIGHT be able to use this as a dress with leggings or tights... I am taller, so that is not happening!  The sleeves are fitted, but not tight in any way - at least on me. I have old lady/plus size lady arms.  :)  You can dress these blouses up or down... depending on your mood... I wear the purple/navy with jeans... the brown/black with a really sparkly necklace and black legging type pants. I bought the first 2 on a "flash" sale.. and I'm going back for 2 more prints. I travel and these blouses are now my super "easy button."  About the material...."made in china" -  tag says..95% rayon, 5% spandex.  This means "delicate wash" on my machine and hang or lay flat to dry.  If you tumble dry your blouse will probably shrink some - and that would be ok for me as I purchased the xx-large.  Honestly,reviews are subjective. If you're on the fence, buy just one and try for yourself.  I bet you'll go back for more too.  I never hesitate to update my reviews if new info would be useful. My husband was looking for something that really does work as carry on and yet, had a roller to save the back.  First you should know that this bag is very big for a "back pack" roller.  It has an expander zipper and could easily be overstuffed, making it difficult to put in overheads.  My husband travels 11 of 12 months of the year, including a lot of international flights.  He finds this bag to be very useful in multiple ways with lots of zippered pouches for travel documents, notepad, files, medications and some clothes.The main compartment opens like a suitcase, side to side, not from the top as you would with a smaller back pack. The rest of the zipper compartments open widely and from the top.  He highly recommends it for a frequent traveler.  I think it would be great for any guy.  I think it would be a bit too big for me though.  Hope this helps!

First off, the regular price for these is ridiculous. I got them on a lightning deal and ultimately paid about $5/pair. A lot more reasonable of a price for a pair of knee highs.  When they arrived.. they are as nice looking as pictured and have a very comfy thickness to the foot bed. I was really disappointed when they didn't fit right. I'll come out and say that I have fat calves, although I prefer the term "athletic". Finding knee high socks is normally not too difficult for me, however the argyle pattern on these specific socks are done so oddly. There are a TON of thread ends inside every sock and the stitching is done so tight that I fear only those who have little to no meat on their bones will be able to wear these comfortably.  I picked one pair and wore them from about 4pm until around 8am the next morning, having slept in them, and I had horrid pain right under my knee. Turns out the upper band is also woven a bit too tight. I took them off and immediately started the return process with Amazon.. had them packaged and handed to my UPS driver later that afternoon. I had deep gouges and red bands encircling my calves, right under my knees for three more days.  So.. these are very cute and extremely comfy! I just wish they didn't have a few yards worth of thread inside them and that they weren't so darn tight! If you have atheletic calves, I wouldn't recommend these. If you have smaller calves, I would highly recommend them as they probably won't cut off circulation on skinnier calves but would be tight enough to stay in place and not fall down.

I am 5'7"... 38DDD and usually wear a 14/16 or 0x/1x... I purchased the 2x after reading other reviews. I am glad I did... the sleeves are none to loose - I think a smaller size might have been a bit snug in the chest and sleeves. The body is PLENTY big. The 2x seems fine though. I've included some pics to help. I chose the black. The jacket is lightly lined, however not overly heavy - it is warm. The zipper has plastic teeth. The pockets are straight (meaning NOT a side slash) and have a flap closure - they have a 1/2 a snap button - for looks and weight (?) - so the pockets do NOT snap closed as there is no second 1/2 to snap into. The hood is double thick - with cotton, knotted pull strings - tie under chin type. There are two side loops - one on each side for the belt. Material tag lists 100% polyester. Also lists, Hand wash.. Medium iron if necessary, Do NOT tumble dry, Do NOT bleach. Lay flat. Made in China. It's nice to be able to choose your closure... zipper... snap... or just tie trench coat style - or all three. The belt is loose/separate... so it' could be possible to loose it. I may sew mine on. SEE BUYER BEWARE section at the end.  UPDATED review posted at the end.  This umbrella is heavy at 1.22 pounds according to my food scale. I'm impressed with how it sheds water and that opening and closing the umbrella is as easy as the press of a button. However, collapsing it to put it in its fabric case requires a bit of strength when pressing the top and bottom together with both arms. This is not the kind of umbrella I'd give to someone who is frail.  I'm actually surprised that the umbrella comes with a lifetime warranty for normal use and freedom from defects and workmanship. Of course, if this is stored wet, the warranty states the frame could rust.  Nevertheless, to get the warranty, it would have to be mailed into to the company in Oregon at the cost of postage. Although the company is in Oregon, this product is made in China.  Below are the PROS and CONS from my perspective.  PROS  - This is medium size for easy carrying and has a full handle which is nice.  - It opens and closes with the touch of a button. I did have it stick once on the automatic close but I just had to press the button again to get it to work.  - The Teflon fabric protector protects from stains not that that matters on a black umbrella. I believe this is what also allows the rain to just run off the fabric.  - When expanded it is full-sized but not super-large like a golf umbrella.  - This comes with a lifetime warranty  - The company has been in business since 1947.  - The fabric includes Scotchlite reflective material which enhances visibility at night and with low light.  I have yet to observe this. Apparently, the Scotchlite material "returns light back to a light source such as vehicle headlights creating an image that motorists are more likely to see from a distance."  CONS  - This umbrella is heavy at 1.22 pounds. It appears most of the weight is in the handle. Also, the handle is a bit big (wide).  - Collapsing the umbrella into its small size after it's automatically closed requires a bit of strength with both arms pushing together.  - It's unclear if this umbrella is a good value as the product and its cost (as of May 10, 2015) is not listed on Amazon.  In summary, I really like this umbrella. I consider this the 2nd best of 5 umbrellas I currently own (including this one). My favorite umbrella is a Windbrella collapsible that I purchased at a flea market for $15. That one is my favorite only because it's very large like a golf umbrella, is just a little bigger than this one when collapsed, and is not as heavy.  However, since I can't get that particular Windbrella anymore, I would consider purchasing this if the price were right (like under $40). I'm waiting for a good windswept rainstorm here to test this out further. I will update my review with the results when we have such weather.  BUYER BEWARE - This review is for the umbrella with ASIN:B00UBNL4EW that has a thick RED handle and not ASIN:B00UBNL97O (which is where this review is showing).  I've tried for over a week to get Amazon to rectify this issue and have been unsuccessful. Therefore, until Amazon corrects the product page be careful that you are ordering the same umbrella that has been reviewed by the Vine Reviewers. Personally, I could not find this umbrella on the Amazon site as of this post. A link to the Amazon picture of the umbrella VINE reviewers received is in the comments section of this review. Just copy the link to your browser to view it.  UPDATE May 16, 2015 - The umbrella worked until my 3rd use when it failed to open and close. The button got completely stuck and my boyfriend even tried it. Although, it has a lifetime warranty, I refuse to pay to have it returned only to get a replacement umbrella that will probably fail. We decided to unscrew the handle to see if we can figure out how to make it work; but the handle won't even come off when the screw is removed even when trying to pry it off.  I see another reviewer had the same problem opening his umbrella except his never worked right. He used a hammer so we decided to try that. We got it to open and close once; then it got stuck. After trying for 10 more minutes, we got it to open and close one more time and then it stuck. My boyfriend finally figured out how to get this to work but it requires effort and is unreliable. First we used all our strength to push the umbrella toward the handle while pressing the button down at the same time. Once the button depresses, we safely pointed the umbrella in the direction we wanted to open it. This usually works.  My boyfriend's recommendation to me is carry a spare umbrella with me because this is so unreliable. Unfortunately, I cannot recommend this particular umbrella.

I am 5'7" .. usually wear a 38DDD/F (I ordered a 40DD in this bra after reading some other reviews)...I chose the nude color.  It is a fairly good fit. But they do still cut into my shoulders (partly because boobs are heavy - eh) ..NO wire - which I know is appealing, BUT it isn't as lifting as a wire - so there's that.  The back strap has 4 hooks and is very wide.  NO buldging under the arm and no fat rolls in the back area - but I think part of that is going up one rib cage size.  The bra is soft and fairly comfortable...The cup coverage is full - but I still had significant cleavage - another challenge of full boobs.  It does have a little lace/pretty at the V of the cleavage - but it is minimal and doesn't show under a Tshirt I tried on with it. So over all it is better than average - but not perfect in my opinion. But lets face it bras are SUPER personal and if you got girls, this one is worth a shot. This wallet is a great size if you don't have to carry a lot of cards and stuff in your wallet. It has 4 card slots plus an ID window. There's a place for bills and another one, albeit quite small, for coins. I like it, but I've been having issues with the snap.  About one out of every three times I try to snap it closed, it won't snap. I have to end up using both hands and applying a lot of pressure and then it usually snaps. I do think that I discovered the problem. The part of the snap that's on the wallet part (not the flap) is loose and rotates easily. If it's lined up a certain way, it snaps fine. If it's not in the right position, then it won't snap closed. One shouldn't have to work that hard to get it closed. I hope that this is just a faulty wallet and it's not happening with others.

This is an absolutely gorgeous and stylish double-breasted woolen trench coat with waist belt. However, unless I am a freak of nature, this coat is not proportionately cut correctly in the arms to fit my size. I ordered the XL and the coat fits my frame snugly but is still good but the arms are very tight. As a result I cannot wear it. I am so disappointed because it is super attractive in the red.  This arrived well packaged in a basic blue garment bag (see picture) made of the material typical of shopping bags that can be purchased at a local supermarket for 99 cents.  LOVES (see 2nd and 3rd picture) - I like the double-breasted look with has a one button closure but two buttons across. - The two slit side pockets are spacious and comfortably angled. - The stand-up collar is super attractive. - The robe-like belt cinches the coat attractively around the waist, almost like a robe but better. - The button detail and the half sash on the wrist is stylish. - The coat feels sufficiently warm. - The interior lining keeps the wool away from the body so that one does not itch.  HATES: - The arms of the coat are not proportionately wide enough for the size of the coat. It seems that an inch or two more girth is needed in that area. This is the only reason I rated this coat two stars.  At the current price of $139.99, I do feel it is about $20 to $40 above what I would pay for this type coat. Although it is sufficiently warm, it is still thinner than a coat of the same type I can buy at Burlington. If the it fit in the arm area, then this would have received a 4 star rating from me at the current price. It would have received 5 stars if the price were between $99 and $120 and it actually fit.  P.S. I am sorry the pictures are on their side. Every time I upload a picture, Amazon changes the orientation even though my orientation is correct. I even tried changing the orientation on my end to be the incorrect way to see if Amazon would self correct to the right-side up view; however, Amazon still turned it into a side image. I spent hours in the past trying to get Amazon to help with this and they cannot seem to correct the issue.  UPDATE: I had my super skinny girlfriend try this on. She said this coat is beautiful and she considers the color crimson, which is a more accurate representation of the color in my opinion. Since the coat is an XL and she takes a small to medium, the arms fit her well but the coat was super huge on her. In conclusion, the girth of the arms are truly not sized correctly for the stated size of the coat. At least they were not sized correctly in the coat I received, so it is off to recycling.

I am 5'10" and about a size 16/18, I typically am a size XL, but like to size up the 2X if possible when it comes to items that have long sleeves or where overall length could be an issue, which I chose to do with this dress. The fabric didn't seem overly stretched out or anything. I got this on sale for around $15 I believe and at that price, I am happy with it. I am not sure that I would willingly spend more than about $20-25 max on it though. The black is nice, the fabric is on the thinner side but isn't terrible and it doesn't look "cheap", but I don't know if I would try my luck with any of the prints though.  If you are right between sizes, or are tall, have long arms,  bigger chest, etc... then I would consider sizing up if possible. But I think you might also be ok with getting your normal size, just depends on your build really.

Without having access to any description, I wasn’t certain what exactly to expect.. I love rubies and the using of mixed metals... I was excited and  expecting this to be a fun, cocktail ring. One I could use going out or, dressing up!  However, when this arrived it was much bigger than the photo provided leads on… It takes up almost one “link” or, at least 1” of my finger!! It is also a tall dome shape, easy to catch on things. Tbh, I think it is just half of a hollowed out circle or, bell. What/Why? This description..??? Well, when flipped over on the underside, it is completely hollow!! I have never seen anything like this. It reminds me a bit like “steampunk” jewelry where they created a style using mixed metals, gears and objects to create a piece of funky jewelry.. only, this feels completely unfinished..?? (The word that comes to mind.) Sizing is not an issue- as it comes on what I refer to as a “childs” adjustable band.. which is a thin and overlapping circle- It’s literally attached by a weld on the underside of the hollowed bell...?? And the ends of the adjustable ring, aren't finished and have sharp squared corners- If you didn’t know this was metal.. you would think this was non-existent, that is how light the material is. Yet, because of the shape.. the ring is top heavy. The use of ruby colored stones is nice.. however, they become lost in the enormity of size with this ring.. honest, I’ve never seen anything like it!  I do however appreciate the box this came in. It is a smaller plastic flip lid box that has secure tab closures.. it would be nice to use for travel as, it’s able to fit a few different pieces of jewelry inside.  I’m sure this description is hard to understand.. But, if I’m being honest- that “hard to understand” describes this ring perfectly!! I did include x3 photos to give some idea of what I’m trying to describe.. As you will see, this is at best- uniquely different and at worst, completely unfinished.  Yes!! This would be a conversation piece of epic proportion‘s only, when they ask to see it for further inspection… I feel it would be almost comical?! I hope this info helped... feel free to let me know!

I am familiar with Kiyonna and always purchase the 2x because I know that's what works for me... I am 5'7"... 38DDD... usually wear a 0x/1x or 14/16.  This dress was no exception.  The 2x fits nicely. A couple of things to note... this is a very form fitting dress in the bust and body area.  The skirt flairs a bit right around thigh area on me... everyone is built a bit differently.. so I encourage you to read the sellers info on sizing.. I do feel they run a bit to the smaller side and sizing up is probably a good idea.  The V is fairly deep.. cleavage could be visible.  however, the lace can be manipulated to be form a bit more modest of a look and even possible stitched a stitch or two to keep it in place. I chose the black lace over black.  The color is deep and not washed out.. a true black in my opinion.  The arms are not lined, as shown in the photo they provide in the ad.  The waist is drawn in a bit.. a tiny bit empire height on my body... silhouette is flattering in my opinion and would be on a lot of different body types.  Being black helps to hide rolls and lumps should you have them.  The gown falls just above the floor for me in bare feet, which I like.  I usually end up hemming things believe it or not - everyone likes things a bit differently.  The dress is a "pull over" meaning - no zippers or buttons. I store it in a garment bag as I don't often need such a formal dress, but just in case it's a beautiful, elegant option that fits like a glove.  I have purchased many Kiyonna items, mostly blouses.  I waited until this dress dropped to my price point and pounced.  :)  I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful

Clifton Heritage normally makes great leather wallets. However, this is not the quality I have come to expect. First of all, I did not expect this to be so rustic. It is made of a hard leather and every nail scrape or pressure smudge shows. It is also not minimalist. This is a regular man's tri-fold wallet that has 11 card slots (if counting the ones behind each bank of slots) and a double billfold. One of the slots is an ID window but that window is so short that the ID must be removed from the slot in order to show it fully. This is a big negative to me.  It supposedly has RFID protection to protect credit cards from scanners, but I have no way of verifying that. It does come in a giftable box but mine came slightly smashed as can be seen in one of the photos.  The leather of this wallet is strong so it will last. The reason I am rating this two stars is because it is not advertised as rustic, it shows scratches, the ID window is too small, and the wallet is not minimalist. It is certainly a useable wallet that feels like it will last a long time and the price of $12.99 is acceptable. Nevertheless, I am disappointed.

It washed in my machine on the 'hand wash' cycle and laid it flat in my dryer closet.  Still, the wrinkles persisted. I will require that I get my steamer or cool iron out to iron. Because I chose the black, the wrinkles are bit less obvious (I think darker colors will hide some wrinkles better) - but beware of the lighter colors - in my opinion.  Overall, I would not purchase again - due to cheap quality.  The style would be so good with a nice quality modal fabric and quality sewing - so it's frustrating but I have to be honest. Having said all of this, I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful. All of my reviews reflect my honest, personal experience with the reviewed item - your experience may be different. I am not influenced by any outside source. I receive/accept NO free products or discounts that are not available to all shoppers- ever. For some reason our shopper ranks are no longer visible - so, to give you a little more info about me, I am a top 50 reviewer (#30 the highest rank achieved). Those numbers used to fluctuate over time - up and down but I noticed that they stopped updating regularly - perhaps to phase them out.  I've been doing reviews for over 25 years with Amazon - nearly 6,000 reviews, viewed over 50,000 times, nearly 24,000 likes.

Loving the two-tone metal… I was excited to have found this ring. However, speaking of metal I’ve never felt anything so light?? Still uncertain how it’s possible- in your hand it feels like air and yet, it is not soft and bendable.. tbh, it reminds me of plumbing pipe that has been cut for industrial use. Remember as kids when you put a washer, a soda ring pull or, any circular item on your finger and pretend it was a ring? Well, this is what i’m reminded of… It’s as light as aluminum but has solid integrity!  The edges are squared off- so, there is no comfort fit in the band which, I was already concerned with the double layers how it could be worn and not irritable.. problem is, I can’t even answer now that I have it in person- due to the fact, sizing is x3 bigger than it is listed as. Knowing for a fact finger size options were 9.5-11, I ordered a 10.. this wont even fit the largest size fingers.. not even close!?  Honestly, I am unsure how this could possibly be used as a ring with both, the lack of sizing issue and comfort in its wear ability? The only option I can see honestly, is if it’s threaded onto a necklace rope/chain and worn around the neck? Possibly could make a unique pendant..?? Unfortunately, as a ring this fall short in its capacity.  I gave this 2 stars for the aesthetic‘s of the 2 contrasting metals together (The look is really unique!) and then, in the hope… If one found their appropriate size maybe they'd have better luck but, that take note: it would be a guessing game at best.. if interested- I’d suggest ordering down a solid 1-1.5 in normal ring size. I hope this info can help your decision… Feel free to let me know!

This Invicta Women's watch has an interesting style.  The big face is the size of a 50-cent coin or slightly larger. The dial is surrounded by "crystals" and is very easy to read.  The "crystals" give the face of the watch a look that would normally be found on a smaller-sized elegant watch.  However, the rest of the watch is very sporty and the band is made of very cheap feeling navy blue plastic.  I like that the watch is very sporty and the color goes exceptionally well with blue jeans.  I love that the dial is also very easy to read. It is easy to set the time by pulling out the crown fully.  To set the date, I just pull out the crown 1/2 way. According to the underside of the watch it is also water resistant up to 30 meters, although I did not test that as water resistant is not the same as waterproof.  The instructions supplied are for many Invicta watches (the quartz, automatic, and manual watches) of many different types (watches with chronographs or ones with just time and date, etc.).  As a result, this made reading the instructions very confusing and a little bit of a treasure hunt to find the information I wanted.  This one is a quartz watch but I could not find anywhere in the instructions what type of battery it takes nor how to get the watch band links off.  There are two things I dislike immensely about this watch besides the fact that it has a big face that is more of a style for a man than a woman.  First, the band feels like it's made of dollar store plastic.  Second, the band is way too long.  I used an eyeglass screwdriver to loosen the first pin on the watch so that I could move the band down inside the clasp as far as it would go; however, the watch is still too big on my wrist and I have what I consider a normal-sized wrist.  I'm going to have to take this to a jewelers to remove about 2 more links.  I think I would have to have about 4 links removed if I wasn't able to move the first pin to a lower position inside the clasp (similar to the way you would move a belt).  I really think the manufacturer should reduce the links by 2 and supply an extra set of 2 links in the package so that someone who is extremely oversized could have them added on.  I think most women would have to go to the watchmaker to remove links rather than add them which adds on to the cost of the watch. Also, if they supplied instructions on how to adjust the links to inside the clasp (like I guessed how to do) it would help most women from having to make any trip to the jewelers at all.  In summary, I love that the face is very easy to read and I find this to be a sporty watch that goes well with jeans.  However, I feel the band is a little too cheap feeling for the current posted price of $79.00.  If I had purchased this watch, I would feel I paid too much at that price.  Personally, with the large band and cheap plastic, I wouldn't pay more than $30 for this watch and even at that price it's a lot.  December 26, 2011 Update - I had a jeweler remove 4 links today at a cost to me of $8. The jeweler also undid my handiwork of moving the links down into the clasp.  January 4, 2012 Update - I've been wearing this for a week and sometimes the band comes undone because the clasp isn't very strong.  This is another disappointment. I also showed a colleague of mine this watch and she said the Invicta watches have a good name.  However, she was very surprised at how this watch resembled junk or something a kid would purchase. She said she probably wouldn't pay more than $20 for it as it looks like a street vendor watch.  However, she did say that it looked pretty from a distance and the band is a nice color blue; both of which I agree with wholeheartedly.  January 12, 2012 Update - I lowered my review from 3 to 2 stars because this watch has too many negatives such as the cheap band, high price listed, wide face, and now the winding screw on the watch has been pinching my wrist. When I bend my wrist the face of the watch is so large that the screw digs in and pinches the skin on the upper part of my hand.  On another note, I also showed another colleague of mine this watch and she said she wouldn't even pay $5 for it.  In her words, "It's just junk."  UPDATE July 2, 2014 - After 2-1/2 years, this watch broke.  The watch has a new battery but it is not keeping time accurately and the second-hand fell off so that now it's floating in the watch. Since I never cared for the cheapness of this watch, I personally do not feel it's worth fixing.

I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful.  Hope this review was helpful to you in some way! UPDATE:  12/10/2018... I have washed and laid flat to dry... just lightly rub and then shake to soften the faux fur back to normal.  Went beautifully.  There was some shedding before laundry - much less after.  I think it will be less and less  - but I will update if not!  I also included a couple of photos... I placed a white tissue on top so you can hopefully get a better idea of the color... As I mentioned above the body is acrylic.... sure wish we could have had a more lux cashmere and pay a little more... but it's ok for as much use as it will get... also I see that I will need to get my steam iron out to remove wrinkles even after laundering. I was initially very excited to receive my pair of these vibrant athletic shorts, but I was woefully underwhelmed with the pair that arrived. The material is so thin that there’s little if any “tummy control” because the fabric isn’t thick enough to provide any sort of notable compression (like the waistband of typical yoga pants would provide). And while the color variation should be a consistently random pattern of purple/red/blue tie-dye, the pair of shorts I received just have one huge blue spot on the side of one leg, so it just looks like an accidental stain instead of an intentional (or attractive) fabric swirl. Some of the stitched edges also exhibit areas of no color at all, so the material quality leaves a lot to be desired. I also felt these ran a little large, which was not at all what I was expecting from a pair of tight athletic shorts. They are stretchy and comfortable, but not too visually flattering.

I am 5'7"... 38DDD and usually wear a 14/16 or 0x/1x.  I purchased this jacket in a 3x because I wanted to CERTAIN I could wear with sweaters and I wanted it to be longer... It is huge.  I will wear it and enjoy it... but I could have done the 1 or 2x I think.  I've included some pics to help.  I chose the black.  It is VERY light - not lined.  The pockets are side slash, with zipper closures - the lining of the pockets is a mesh material. The hood is hidden in a velcro'd collar flap - the hood itself has elastic pull cords.  There is a Columbia logo on the left upper chest. The sleeves are very long - and have velcro's strips to adjust fit around your wrist.  Because I purchased the really huge size, the jacket falls well below my back end.  All of that is ok with me!  The washing instructions say cold water, powdered detergent, , wash separately - Do not bleach.  Tumble dry on low.  Do NOT use fabric softener and Do NOT dry clean.  Lists 100% nylon - except mesh... 100% polyester.  I haven't worn it in a rain storm yet, but it looks like it will work well for Spring in Oregon.  Having said all of that, I never hesitate to update my reviews should new info seem useful and I hope this review was helpful to you.

I know this blazer is meant to be oversized but it is EXTREMELY oversized. I am short (5 feet 4 inches) and the length of the blazer and sleeves are just way too long. I even sized down one and it was still too big. It just looks unprofessional. I wouldn’t wear it to work. A bit disappointed.

One of the nicer watches I own is a Skagen watch and I was excited to have the opportunity to work with this one. It’s a nice watch – when it runs.  This watch is a beautiful skeleton watch with a blue face and a lovely view of the innards of the case. It seems to keep good time so far, though I have more to say on that in a minute. The band is a stainless steel and is adjustable. My first impression of the clasp was bad as I didn’t see how it worked and I could not get it to clasp properly; once I figured it out I realized it actually is more secure and is less likely to unsnap and slip off unexpectedly which is a good thing.  Twice in a week, this watch has simply stopped, with no indication of why. It simply shut off and quit keeping time. If I turn the spindle it starts running again, but this is supposed to be a battery-powered watch, not a manual winding or self winding watch, so this is a major issue. It runs for a few hours and keeps perfect time while doing so, but then stops again.  Also, when I wear the watch there is a faint rattle to it that I have traced to the case, not the band or clasp. It sounds very much like something has come loose inside, which does not bode well.  I like Skagen watches. But, I don’t like this one. For me, in spite of how attractive it is and how well the band is designed, it is unreliable and that’s a bad attribute for a watch. I assume I got a dud, but I can’t rate it higher based on an assumption.  I plan to contact the manufacturer to see if I can get warranty service. If so, I will come back and update this review.
